symptoms.symptom_name: Severe, long-standing fear localised specifically to needles

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: specific (needle) phobia with situational panic attack
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An intense fear bound specifically to a single stimulus (needles) is the defining feature of a specific phobia.
Stimulus-specificity argues against pervasive, free-floating GAD.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute panic symptoms (pounding heart, hyperventilation, shaking, tearfulness) triggered by the needle, occurring before any injection

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: specific (needle) phobia with situational panic attack
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A stimulus-bound sympathetic surge is the expected autonomic response of a situational panic attack.
The arousal precedes any vaccine and lacks urticaria, angioedema and airway/circulatory involvement — it cannot be anaphylaxis, which requires exposure to the allergen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Otherwise well, afebrile, symptoms confined to the needle situation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute febrile / infective illness precluding vaccination
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever or infective symptoms clears the patient for vaccination and excludes an acute-illness deferral.

symptoms.symptom_name: No previous severe allergic reaction to a vaccine or its components

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No history of vaccine-component anaphylaxis lowers the baseline risk of a true allergic reaction — though post-vaccination observation is still mandatory.

symptoms.symptom_name: History of near-fainting with previous injections (vasovagal predisposition)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: specific (needle) phobia with situational panic attack
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Blood-injection-injury phobia is characteristically associated with vasovagal syncope, supporting the phobic diagnosis and dictating safe positioning at administration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (BP 140/85, HR 112, RR 20, Temp 36.6, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: specific (needle) phobia with situational panic attack
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild hypertension and tachycardia with a normal temperature and full oxygen saturation are consistent with acute anxiety — a sealed clinician measurement, not obtainable via chat, and not a marker of an emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hepatitis B surface antibody titre (post-immunisation serology to confirm occupational immunity)

investigation_details.expected_result: Protective anti-HBs titre developing weeks after administration, confirming occupational immunity
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Occupational immunity is not documented; a routine GP review at ~4 weeks arranges the titre.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: need for occupational Hepatitis B immunisation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A protective antibody titre confirms the immunisation achieved occupational immunity — a laboratory result never available in real time via telehealth.
